commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer product/compatibility approval for default schema sidecar generation and root $schema stamping.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain issue was found in the config, docs, script allowlist, or lint-suppression changes. (sha=c777d5a6269bec0845cd52293ea58c3c895cc75e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates OpenClaw config writes to refresh `openclaw.schema.json`, stamp a local root `$schema` reference on root-owned writes, document the behavior, and cover the root/include-backed write paths with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +94, Tests +294, Docs +10, Other 0. Total +398 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as an issue reproduction; this is a feature PR. The PR body provides an isolated `openclaw config set` run showing the resulting config value, `$schema` reference, and sidecar schema fields.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer product/compatibility approval for default schema sidecar generation and root `$schema` stamping.

target.title: feat(config): write local editor schema
target.body: I don’t seem to be able to touch my `openclaw.json` without breaking something, and it’s hard to discover all the available options. This PR adds a small breadcrumb for humans and our agents: when OpenClaw writes config, it also leaves behind the local schema editors need for validation and IntelliSense. ## What changed - Writes or refreshes `openclaw.schema.json` next to the active `openclaw.json` after OpenClaw-owned config writes. - Adds `"$schema": "./openclaw.schema.json"` only when the root config file is already being committed and no non-empty schema is present. - Keeps existing non-empty `$schema` values alone. - Respects explicit `$schema` removal; `openclaw config unset '$schema'` does not immediately add it back. - Keeps include-backed writes root-preserving: the included fragment is updated, the schema sidecar is refreshed best-effort, and the root config is not rewritten just to add `$schema`. - Reuses the same schema helper for `openclaw config schema` and the editor-facing schema file. ## Real behavior proof - Behavior addressed: OpenClaw-owned config writes create a local editor schema, add a root `$schema` reference when the root file is naturally rewritten, preserve include-backed roots, and do not let editor-schema side effects break the requested config write. - Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux, with isolated temporary `HOME` / `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` values so this did not touch my real `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: ```bash tmp_home=$(mktemp -d) mkdir -p "$tmp_home/.openclaw" config="$tmp_home/.openclaw/openclaw.json" printf '{"gateway":{"mode":"local"}}\n' > "$config" HOME="$tmp_home" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$config" OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 \ node scripts/run-node.mjs config set gateway.port 18790 --strict-json ``` - Evidence after fix: terminal output from the isolated runtime write showed that the config was updated, a local schema reference was present, and the schema sidecar existed with expected root fields: ```json { "schemaRef": "./openclaw.schema.json", "port": 18790, "schemaFileExists": true, "hasGateway": true, "hasChannels": true, "hasSchemaProperty": { "type": "string" } } ``` VS Code also picks up the local schema and shows IntelliSense: - Observed result after fix: `openclaw.json` was updated with the requested config value and `"$schema": "./openclaw.schema.json"`, and `openclaw.schema.json` existed beside it with expected root schema fields. The follow-up code cleanup keeps include-backed writes root-preserving while still refreshing the sidecar best-effort. - What was not tested: Nix-managed config writes, because that path is intentionally immutable and config writers refuse Nix-mode writes. ## Include-backed compatibility proof Final contract after the holistic cleanup: - Include-backed single-top-level writes update the include target only. - The root config remains byte-for-byte unchanged; this avoids raw JSON5 root surgery and preserves comments/formatting. - `openclaw.schema.json` is still refreshed best-effort beside the root config. - If sidecar generation fails, the requested include-backed write still succeeds. Regression coverage: - `src/config/mutate.test.ts`: proves an include-backed `plugins.json5` write refreshes the editor schema without rewriting the root config. - `src/config/mutate.test.ts`: simulates an `EACCES` editor-schema failure and proves the include-backed write still commits while the root remains unchanged. - `src/config/io.write-config.test.ts`: simulates an `EACCES` sidecar write failure and proves the primary `openclaw.json` write still persists. ## Explicit unset proof Contract: - Ordinary missing or blank root `$schema` values are stamped when the root config is being committed. - If a config write explicitly includes `unsetPaths: [["$schema"]]`, the writer does not add `$schema` back. Regression coverage: - `src/config/io.write-config.test.ts`: writes a config with `$schema`, performs a write with `unsetPaths: [["$schema"]]`, and verifies the persisted `openclaw.json` no longer has `$schema`. ## Tested ```bash node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/io.write-config.test.ts src/config/mutate.test.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-heartbeat-template-repair.test.ts src/agents/workspace-templates.test.ts src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts corepack pnpm exec oxlint src/config/editor-schema.ts src/config/io.ts src/config/io.write-prepare.ts src/config/mutate.ts src/config/io.write-config.test.ts src/config/mutate.test.ts src/commands/doctor-heartbeat-template-repair.test.ts node --import tsx scripts/generate-base-config-schema.ts --check corepack pnpm config:schema:check git diff --check ``` ## Notes - Nix-managed config is still intentionally immutable; this does not change that path. - Manual edits do not refresh the schema by themselves. The schema is refreshed when OpenClaw owns the config write. - The main review question is now just product/compatibility acceptance of the local schema sidecar and root `$schema` on root-owned writes.
target.author: danhayman

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

The follow-up code cleanup keeps include-backed writes root-preserving while still refreshing the sidecar best-effort. - What was not tested: Nix-managed config writes, because that path is intentionally immutable and config writers refuse Nix-mode writes. ## Include-backed compatibility proof Final contract after the holistic cleanup: - Include-backed single-top-level writes update the include target only. - The root config remains byte-for-byte unchanged; this avoids raw JSON5 root surgery and preserves comments/formatting. - `openclaw.schema.json` is still refreshed best-effort beside the root config. - If sidecar generation fails, the requested include-backed write still succeeds. Regression coverage: - `src/config/mutate.test.ts`: proves an include-backed `plugins.json5` write refreshes the editor schema without rewriting the root config. - `src/config/mutate.test.ts`: simulates an `EACCES` editor-schema failure and proves the include-backed write still commits while the root remains unchanged. - `src/config/io.write-config.test.ts`: simulates an `EACCES` sidecar write failure and proves the primary `openclaw.json` write still persists. ## Explicit unset proof Contract: - Ordinary missing or blank root `$schema` values are stamped when the root config is being committed. - If a config write explicitly includes `unsetPaths: [["$schema"]]`, the writer does not add `$schema` back. Regression coverage: - `src/config/io.write-config.test.ts`: writes a config with `$schema`, performs a write with `unsetPaths: [["$schema"]]`, and verifies the persisted `openclaw.json` no longer has `$schema`. ## Tested ```bash node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/io.write-config.test.ts src/config/mutate.test.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-heartbeat-template-repair.test.ts src/agents/workspace-templates.test.ts src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts corepack pnpm exec oxlint src/config/editor-schema.ts src/config/io.ts src/config/io.write-prepare.ts src/config/mutate.ts src/config/io.write-config.test.ts src/config/mutate.test.ts src/commands/doctor-heartbeat-template-repair.test.ts node --import tsx scripts/generate-base-config-schema.ts --check corepack pnpm config:schema:check git diff --check ``` ## Notes - Nix-managed config is still intentionally immutable; this does not change that path. - Manual edits do not refresh the schema by themselves. The schema is refreshed when OpenClaw owns the config write. - The main review question is now just product/compatibility acceptance of the local schema sidecar and root `$schema` on root-owned writes.
target.author: danhayman
